title: Syncing Audio and Timeline Basics

video.note: Introduces audio sync using waveform matching, switching to the Edit page, source viewer versus timeline viewer, adding clips to a timeline, playhead movement, track basics, and waveform awareness.

learningTarget: I can create a simple Resolve timeline, place clips in order, and explain the difference between source clips and the timeline edit.

bellRinger.prompt: Why might camera audio and separate microphone audio need to be matched before editing?

vocabulary.definition: The process of matching separate audio and video so dialogue or sound lines up correctly.
A visual shape that shows the loud and quiet parts of audio.
The Resolve page used to build and refine a story in a timeline.
The viewer used to preview a clip before adding it to the timeline.
The viewer that shows the edited sequence at the playhead.
The marker that shows the current time position in a clip or timeline.
A horizontal lane in the timeline that holds video or audio clips.

slides.title: Lesson 03 - Syncing Audio and Timeline Basics

assignment.title: First Timeline Assembly
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of a timeline with at least four clips.
Google Drive or Docs link labeling source viewer, timeline viewer, playhead, and tracks.
Short reflection explaining one timeline decision.

exitTicket: How do the source viewer and timeline viewer help with different editing decisions?
